zh.lifeSummary: 當Ketel fitz Eldred於大約1069年出生在英國波藍德時，他的父親Eldred16歲，母親Beatrix de Taillebois10歲。他於1087年與Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德肯達爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1150年在英國維斯特莫藍德肯達爾享年83歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ketel fitz Eldred于大约1069年出生在英国波藍德时，他的父亲Eldred16岁，母亲Beatrix de Taillebois10岁。他于1087年与Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德肯達爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1150年在英国維斯特莫藍德肯達爾享年83岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Eldred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Aldred  . This form of the surname is widespread in eastern England, especially in Essex.

variants.name: Aldred
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Al(d)red or El(d)red, an overlap or merger of three Old English names, Ealdrǣd (or Aldrǣd), Æthelrǣd, and Ælfrǣd. Old English Ealdrǣd and its variant Aldrǣd regularly appear in Middle English as either Eldred or Aldred, depending on dialect. The pronunciation of Old English Æthelrǣd was often shortened, occurring in Middle English as Ailred, Eilred, Alred, and Elred. Subsequently a transitional -d- was inserted between the l and the r, resulting in A(i)ldred and E(i)ldred. Middle English forms of Old English Ælfrǣd sometimes lost the medial -f-, becoming Alred or Elred, and then Aldred, Eldred. All three personal names were largely obsolete by the end of the 13th century.
variant of   Audrey   2.
English:

nameMeanings.name: Ketel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Kjetil .

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Ketel fitz Eldred was born about 1069, in Cumberland, England, his father, Eldred, was 16 and his mother, Beatrix de Taillebois, was 10. He married Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal in 1087, in Kendal, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died about 1150, in Kendal, Westmorland, England, at the age of 83, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ketel fitz Eldred est né environ 1069, à Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, Eldred, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Beatrix de Taillebois, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal en 1087, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé environ 1150, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ketel fitz Eldred um 1069 in Cumberland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Eldred 16 und seine Mutter Beatrix de Taillebois 10. Er hat 1087 in Kendal, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb um 1150 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Kendal, Westmorland, England und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ketel fitz Eldred è nato il circa 1069 nel Cumberland, England, il suo padre, Eldred, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Beatrix de Taillebois, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal nel 1087, nel Kendal, Westmorland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì circa 1150, nel Kendal, Westmorland, England, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolto a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Ketel fitz Eldredが約1069年にイギリスクウンバーランドで生まれた時，父Eldredは16歳，母Beatrix de Tailleboisは10歳であった。1087年にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドケンダルでChristiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は約1150年にイギリスウエストモーランドケンダルで83歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ketel fitz Eldred(이)가 대략 1069년 영국 c벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Eldred(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrix de Taillebois(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1087년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1150년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ketel fitz Eldred nasceu aproximadamente 1069, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Eldred, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix de Taillebois, tinha 10 anos. Ele casou-se com Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal em 1087, em Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1150, em Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, com 83 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ketel fitz Eldred родился около 1069 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Eldred было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrix de Taillebois, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal в 1087 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер около 1150 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия в возрасте 83 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

sm.about 1069–about 1150: e tusa o le 1069–e tusa o le 1150

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ketel fitz Eldred nació aproximadamente 1069, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Eldred, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Beatrix de Taillebois, tenía 10 años. Se casó con Christiana de Taillebois Baroness of Kendal en 1087, en Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1150, en Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
